questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of any quadrilateral?
How many diagonals does a quadrilateral have?
What is the condition for a quadrilateral to be a parallelogram using diagonals?
questions.answer: If the diagonals bisect each other.

questions.question: What is the relationship between adjacent angles of a parallelogram?
What happens to the diagonals of a parallelogram?
What is the special property of diagonals of a rectangle?
What is the special property of diagonals of a rhombus?
questions.answer: They bisect each other at right angles.

questions.question: What is the property of diagonals of a square?
questions.answer: They are equal, perpendicular and bisect each other.

questions.question: How many pairs of opposite sides are parallel in a parallelogram?
If one angle of a parallelogram is 90°, what is the shape?
If all sides of a parallelogram are equal, what is it called?
If a quadrilateral has one pair of parallel sides, what is it called?
What property of parallelogram proves opposite sides are equal?
questions.answer: Opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal and parallel.

questions.question: How many congruent triangles are formed when both diagonals of a parallelogram are drawn?
What happens when one diagonal is drawn in a parallelogram?
questions.answer: It divides the parallelogram into two congruent triangles.

questions.question: What is the angle between diagonals of a rhombus?
Which quadrilateral is both a rectangle and a rhombus?
What property proves a quadrilateral is a parallelogram using opposite sides?
questions.answer: If both pairs of opposite sides are equal.

questions.question: If diagonals of a quadrilateral bisect each other, what is the quadrilateral?
Which quadrilateral has perpendicular diagonals but not necessarily equal diagonals?
Which quadrilateral has equal diagonals but not perpendicular diagonals?
What is the sum of adjacent angles of a parallelogram?
If all sides and all angles of a quadrilateral are equal, what is it called?
Which quadrilateral has diagonals that are perpendicular bisectors of each other?
What is the special property of diagonals of a square?
questions.answer: They are equal, perpendicular and bisect each other.
